steps.title: Track Spanish's role
steps.content: The passage says cocoa came to English indirectly through Spanish after Spanish borrowed it from Nahuatl. It also says puma entered English through Spanish from Quechua. In both examples, Spanish is the intermediary.

steps.title: Reject unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice B compares Spanish's contribution with Indigenous languages, but the passage gives no such measure. Choice C invents equal numbers of adopted words. Choice D says Spanish contributed words to Indigenous languages, reversing the direction described. Spanish served as a medium between Indigenous languages and English. The answer is A.
